implementationDetails.description: Added CSS custom property override to lock CardsCarousel progress bar and navigation button gradients to neon-green-to-blue color scheme, regardless of page accent color
implementationDetails.approach: Scoped override using CSS custom properties - the existing .swiper-pagination-progressbar-fill and .carousel-nav-btn::before rules already reference --gradient-left and --gradient-right, so they automatically inherit the locked values when inside .cards-carousel scope

designDecisions.scopedOverride: Used .cards-carousel class scope to only affect CardsCarousel, not other carousel blocks like TestimonialsCarousel which should still respect page accent color
designDecisions.cssTokens: Used var(--color-neon-green) and var(--color-blue) for maintainability instead of raw rgba values
designDecisions.gradientPreserved: Maintained the neon-green-to-blue gradient effect (two colors) rather than locking to solid neon-green

qaChecklist: Verify progress bar gradient stays neon-green-to-blue on pages with .blue, .orange, .purple, .yellow, or .green accent color classes
Verify navigation button hover fill gradient stays neon-green-to-blue on all accent color pages
Verify other carousel blocks (TestimonialsCarousel, DynamicContentCarousel) still respect page accent color
Verify dark mode still works correctly for CardsCarousel
Test in both editor and frontend views
